rule_doc_snippets.monday_block: A fixed time slot reserved for all academics (Monday from 11:00 to 12:00), during which no lectures are scheduled
rule_doc_snippets.daily_cap: General courses should be scheduled at times suitable for students across all levels/semesters, depending on instructor availability (e.g., Communication I taught by Omar Albarno from 9:00 to 15:00). The maximum teaching load per academic per day is 6 hours
rule_doc_snippets.student_credit_cap: Maximum course load per student is 23 credits
rule_doc_snippets.puc_min: PUC students must register for at least 12 credits during regular semesters
rule_doc_snippets.self_funded_range: Self-funded students may register between 3 and 23 credits
rule_doc_snippets.cp_iawd_split: CP and IAWD courses are split between lecture and lab (4 credits = 2 hours lecture + 2 hours lab)
rule_doc_snippets.merged_courses: Some courses have dual codes (merged courses) and must be scheduled in the same hall at the same time (e.g., ACC0014 & ACC2201; see attached list)
rule_doc_snippets.cohort_no_conflict: Courses each semester should be scheduled in a way that avoids conflicts for students following the major sheet plan
rule_doc_snippets.room_capacity: Match the number of students in each section with the capacity of the assigned hall to ensure suitability.
rule_doc_snippets.independent_sections: Ability to create customized schedules for students who require independent sections

equivalencies_summary.note: Transfer-credit data; not used by V0 scheduler.

notes: Room registry is hardcoded — rooms do not change term-to-term.
Aliases capture every spelling observed in SIS schedules (B2-04 ≡ B2-004, "Maple Leaf" ≡ A2-147, etc).

reports.source: Courses Details.xlsx (+ Course Descriptions.docx as reference)
notes: Course rows deduplicated by course code; programs concatenated.
Lecture pattern derived from credits + course-code prefix; CST/CP 4-cr courses default to lab+lecture.
Languages: 'Bilangual' (sic) and 'English & Arabic' both map to ['en','ar'].
Reference text only; not used by the solver. Indexed by course code.
